content: The Helot with their name marked in white with :Catena is an Alternate Version which generally means a stronger version of the original Character
Ok, thx
Yh ur wc

suggestion for a wheel to max?

I'm generally thinking between Amidst the Downpour (Faros, free strike + dmg amp), Chains Unbound (Helot, base dmg + dmg amp), Bloody Feast (Uvhash, Exalt crit + crit dmg) but if you think another one is good i'm interested in the reasoning too
i was thinking whether I should go back to standard awakener banner but then I don't think it's anywhere near as good
If you got Kath, there's an argument for raising Will Unyielding (Alva's WoD)
I sadly don't have Kath, though yeah I haven't been thinking about how that one is usable for her since she just want any of her command
I know about the Medicine tech, but I thought Will Unyielding was kinda unusable because it floods your deck
was thinking more in the line of... Castor who want any of his card
it's my least used DR card due to the additional card to deck lol

It raises the Wielder's Damage & Shield Output, grants additional Copies of their base Strike & Defense, on top of raising their base Strike Crit Rate & DMG (one-time) after playing base Defense as well as raises their Shield Generation from base Defense after playing a base Strike. The Tech for base Defense into base Strike Crit can be used up to 3 times and the Tech for base Strike into base Defense Shield Generation can be stacked up to 3 times (if I'm reading all of this correctly)

yeah i know it's really good for kath specifically, but there's an argument about how it's not very good on most of the awakeners (the other exception i was thinking about is castor specifically)

because you're either:
- strikers (people whose command cards are considered "strike") -> you don't generally want to play a lot of Defense, and the value of Defense is not that high.
- "normal" awakeners -> you don't play Strike unless it has additional effects because it deal like, no damage; or you want the Aliemus
- defender -> you don't generally want to play Strike (no damage, only aliemus or special effect if any).

so you are kinda stuck with awakeners who always want at least one of their command cards no matter what, and want to use both their basic strike and defense (which i can think of alva, castor, kathiruga)
Oh yeah, that's fair. While it does provide Death Resistance, the additional Copies of base Strike & Defense can flood your Deck
like it does not just require you to "play their strike / defense"
it require you to play multiple
for the 15% additional thing to even proc
so like strikers get a 15% buff every once in a while if you want to spend 1 arith NOT on a strike
Oh, yeah, I see where you're coming from. I was also thinking more in line of (If you have Kath, Will Unyielding can be one of your WoD Options to raise to +12)
yeah, but I don't have kath, so i was thinking about it in terms of generic DR wheel lol
Definitely... not versatile
Not many Awakeners want to play both their base Strike & Defense (at all times.) Eats away at your Arith and also floods your Deck with additional Copies of base Strike & Defense

once you've finished titan, the answer to "which standard wheel should I max next" is predicated upon answering the prerequisite question of "which character do I plan to invest in most over the next month or two"
there are no more wheels so good that you should drop everything and prioritize them immediately
Ohhhh
Yeah, when you put it that
only wheels that are great for specific characters, thus which wheel you want relies on which character you want to focus on next
It makes alot of sense now
i mean yeah, incalc was my designated wheel for thais
so i guess that make sense
but now i'm thinking more in a line of
I have way too many SSR wheels
yeah like incalc standalone isn't even really all that amazing, but it's the best wheel for thais and thus implicitly kind of becomes the default second for most people
and not nearly enough SR ones
so which one am I going to be able to utilize
in Dtides
the easiest thing to do is to map out the main teams you currently use or plan to use in dtide and figure out where the holes that need filling are
Which WoD gives me the most bang for my buck and can possibly be used on the most amount of Awakeners?
I mean you can kind of argue that the 3 wheels i put in my initial message are sorta good on most of the cast, if not all though
admist, chains, feast

do you have a team that's almost viable but is missing a dr proc or two to feel consistent? go for dr wheel
do you have a team that's almost but not quite a5 viable and you think throwing a second ssr on a support would do it? get whatever fits the main support, etc
most people prefer that over any SR either due to the raw stat, or the effect
or both
downpour is (imo) the best generally-good standard wheel left but there are a lot of situations and teams where i don’t use it
i think just having amp and refund is good enough for 80% of team and 100% of dtides (as in, it will be used in all and any dtide)
there's no way it can't fit in any of the 5 teams

stat wise, keyflare and dr are so overpoweringly strong that it becomes difficult to justify amp investment outside of particular scalers or damage comps (and ofc those are the ones that want lighthouse)
When does Prototype Horizon unlock in the shop?
but there are no keyflare standards and the standard dr wheels are more niche
start of month
the 1st of each month specifically
resets every month

I was wondering why it wasn't there
<t:1771290000:t> <- this in terms of your timezone

at +6 / +3 / e3 respectively
but if not then i'd really only aim for lighthouse if you often use clem, castor, or play poison or one of the more niche basedmg-specific setups
ah if you have bp wheels then it's very hard to argue against lighthouse

lighthouse+birth is ridiculously strong

as her exalt give more sacrifice than shield

anyway
her waltz is good card
abyss hymn good card
exalt good
in lemurian team, gmurphy uses exalt and waltzes to deal damage, miryam is aliemus support, goliath and tulu temporary buffer for burst damage turn
any exalt triggers gmurphy to make a shield, that's the main source of sustain
what you normally do is OE gmurphy at the start of the fight, stall a bit, then everyone exalts and gmurphy drops a giant damage nuke
Without OE her maximum burst damage is much more limited

tell me how to use Pollux & Castor in battle? I'm filling my other two slots with Horla and Thais. If there are any articles explaining their usage, please share them. I don't have Clem or Gdoll.
is ur pollux e3 or above
I'm E0, but I can increase it to E3.
Do you like his gameplay?
I don't know because I've never used it.

Pollux wants to play a card every turn, but doesn't care if it's the first card in the turn. His first card will gain a huge buff, and this works with defense as well. At e0, this is basically it until you exalt
When you exalt, you get a sacred heart card. This card returns to the top of the deck when played and on the third play buffs pollux's next card
play him first
Unfortunately, his playstyle at e0 is quite boring
it isnt the matter of dupes, its about first impression
so he can make a choice if he wants to invest or not
prolly one of the best dps if E3, but make sure you try his gameplay first if it suits you
He asked about e0 pollux I answered how to play e0 pollux
but he can make it E3 if he wants to
I'd like to try using it in the 'xpress' event because it seems strong, so once I understand how to use them, I'll invest in it.
yes borrow pollux  whale
Basically for E3-whale pollux, you let castor ult and gather onyx plumes (0 cost), then alternate it with sacred heart (1 cost) to make his Cleansing judgement (3 cost) become 0 cost

Hello, I literally started playing Morimens today and I want to begin on the right path. I have a lot of experience with gacha games like Honkai and Zenless zone zero, so I know what kind of game this is. I’d like to know things such as team compositions, which characters are the most important, and which ones I should try to get

You’re not going to be able to get a satisfactory answer with a question like that.
The parameters are too vague.

I could give you ten endgame team compositions right off the bat and you’ll only be able to build one of them.
Same concept with units and everything.
Shouldn’t be looking at the big picture but rather be taking things one banner at a time
Just use characters you like and have fun rn
Don’t worry mate, I just saw the guides
And now he’s cooked<:dead:1356082627509092372>
i have a guide for some strong units but it won't help you that much since it's for +3 month players

experience with HSR/ZZZ isn't going to help much because most mainstream gacha games have very clearly defined teamcomps where characters are built with specific synergies, so any given attacker character will likely only have 2-3 viable endgame supports for them because they've been designed that way

that doesn't really apply in this game because this game is more of a freeform card game. it has general archetypes and rough roles (i.e., there will be characters that are strong defensively that you use for defense, or characters that are strong offensively that you use as a carry), but in general you can take literally any team, choose one of the slots, and find half a dozen other characters that are viable switch-ins for that slot
-# (there are a few exceptions to this as there always is, but they are very much exceptions and not the rule)
Holy yap
but ur cooking

in terms of characters that are the most important, the answer is Thais. no other characters realistically matter because she is the only S-tier universal support that can work to some degree on every team in the game

in terms of characters that you should try to get, the answer is Thais. aside from that, find one damage dealer that you like and stick to it, that'll get you the furthest for the least effort

no, there's plenty of characters in the roster to fill out 5 teams (for older accounts that have everything)
subpar slot issues really only start popping up if you're going for full 10 team runs
and yeah what frost said
Hum
like if you need a healer/sustain character, you have lily, gdoll, clem, salv, leigh, depending on realm and comp
all of which do other things as well but just purely on a survival aspect
and that's just off the top of my head, i'm sure others could list half a dozen more i missed
now obviously for specific niches and teams, certain characters are going to be more optimal than others
I don't mean filling teams, but actual op chars. If we have 5 thais, wouldn't almost every team be running her? Other than GMurphy ofc

but you see people running niche and less used characters all the time just because they provide very specific interactions or support that they're looking for specifically even over more common meta options
well yeah but that's thais, she's in her own special world
if you take a very generic everyone-agrees-this-character-is-good-but-not-broken support like, say, idk, ramona
you're probably not seeing 5 ramonas in dtide
but you will probably see one or two from some people and zero from others

If you want to play an optimal team with caro, you run thais

if dtide had no usage restrictions ever, you'd still see ramona show up even when other options exist

I just looked at some arc 2 chars and saw GMurphy and Xu and Pollux, which basically want to play a certain comp, and got the impression that we're just missing chars to be the bis supps for the different archetypes
someone did h4-10 with non-goliath faros back in like the first couple weeks with lvl60 supports and +4 gmurphy!!!
it's viable!!!
We are currently in a conversation about the diff between viability and optimization
well pollux is far more arguable, there's some variety in the stuff he runs
castor is just the easiest and gets used most commonly
gmurphy is a newer char that has shoehorned teamcomps yes, she's an exception but even then there's debate between the slots
Well even Xu, yes you run poison u run Xu but the other units in a Xu comp get a lot of discussiona s well
Some say Liz others Nymph some forget both and just run GDoll
That's what happens in other games too though, the argument was comparing this to HSR and Zzz. I play hsr, and chars have optimal teams, but they can fit other supps anyway

xu is meta poison support and thus almost always used in poison yes, but you have monchi who literally did double poison 425 this dtide with only one xu and poison has always been a niche if not meta strat that's cleared all content in the game even before xu came out
Yeah and all were saying is that while there is 1 usually 2 bis supports for a unit team comps aren't truly static
Not for most teams
Now that would change if there were 5 Thais but thats because Thais is a poorly designed unit
I understand that, I'm just saying that I felt that happens because they still didn't release the bis supps for the last slot or two
The fact that we need 5 teams means there are more archetypes here, and we still didn't get 4 broken limited chars for each archetype

Like poison didn't have great limited supps. Now that Xu released, if someone wants to play poison, 1 of the slots is set. When they release a great limited poison dps, that slot will be set too. And then another supp and a sustain
